sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The agent moved DOWN 3 rows (from row 30-31 to row 36-38, but only 3 DOWN actions were executed out of 6 requested). The blocks stayed at rows 13, 19, 25. So blocks don't move when the agent moves DOWN.
Wait, the agent is now at row 36-38. The initial position was row 36-41. So the agent is close to the initial position.
Now the agent is at row 36-38, and the blocks are at rows 13, 19, 25. The path would be at rows 38-39 (below the agent). The nearest block (N) is at rows 25-28. So the path is still below all blocks.
But wait, looking at the image, the agent is now BELOW the left-side markers. The markers are at rows 14-29, and the agent is at row 36-38. So the agent is below the marker region.
Now, let me think about this differently. What if the left-side markers are the "target" positions, and I need to move the blocks to match them? The markers are at rows 14-15 (g), 16-19 (G), 20-21 (g), 22-25 (G), 26-27 (g), 28-29 (G). These are 6 pairs of markers, each pair 3-4 rows apart.
The blocks are at rows 13-16 (R), 19-22 (b), 25-28 (N). Each block is 3 rows tall and 3 cols wide.
The markers seem to define "slots" for blocks. Each slot is 3-4 rows tall. There are 6 slots but only 3 blocks.
Actually, looking more carefully, the markers alternate: g, G, g, G, g, G. Maybe the g slots are for one set of blocks and G slots are for another? Or maybe the markers just indicate the boundaries of the play area.
OK, I need to try a completely different approach. Let me look at the MOUSE action. Maybe I need to click on the blocks to rearrange them.
Actually, let me try clicking on the blocks in the bottom panel to see if that does anything. The bottom panel shows the target order: R, N, b. Maybe I need to click on the blocks in the right panel in the order shown in the bottom panel.
Let me try: MOUSE click on red block, then green, then blue.

- Agent now at row 36-38, near initial position
- Blocks locked at rows 13, 19, 25 (R, b, N)
- Maybe need to use MOUSE to interact with blocks
